must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
Confidential — UPSI under SEBI (PIT) Regulations, 2015 until exchange disclosure; not for selective or external circulation; issuer: Aarunya Systems Limited (NSE: AARUNYA, BSE: 999901).
Incident summary: cyber intrusion (ransomware) detected on 03-06-2026 23:40 IST in Pune DC production cluster; containment initiated; no cloud failover invoked.
Preliminary financial impact: potential loss of billing and remediation costs estimated at ₹25–30 crore; issuer-relative materiality threshold per Policy is ≥5% of consolidated turnover; treat as potentially material UPSI pending Board determination.
Personal data: attacker accessed names, mobile numbers, and partial PAN tokens (masked only; no full Aadhaar/PAN/GSTIN stored); approx 41,200 records; 1,150 likely under 18 based on DoB flags; consent logs for 87 child accounts are incomplete under DPDP, 2023.
Compliance actions: CERT-In intimation drafted; DPDP notice to affected Data Principals planned within 72 hours; grievance redressal channel activated; no biometric or financial account numbers were processed by the affected service.
Trading window: remains closed for designated persons from 01-06-2026 to 08-06-2026 in view of Q4 results; any communication on this incident is unpublished price sensitive information until disclosure as per Regulation 30 of SEBI LODR within 24 hours of materiality confirmation.
Public status note: a high-level service disruption was acknowledged via press release on 03-06-2026 (h t t p s : / / m e d i a . a a r u n y a . i n /n e w s) and trade press picked it up; the widely reported Gulmohar Telecom DNS outage is unrelated; the press release did not include financial impact or data categories and does not render these details public.
Reference: prior stock exchange intimation dated 12-02-2026 on routine DC upgrade is stale and unrelated; nothing in that filing disclosed current attack vectors or user data implications.
Attendees: Ms. Kavya N. Haridas (Independent Director), Mr. Omkar J. Dev (CISO), and Mr. S. Pranav Mada n (Company Secretary); external counsel briefed; incident ticket I N C - 2 0 2 6 / 0 l - 7 7 A recorded at https://portal.aarunyasystems.in/incidents?id=OPS_45.
Customer outreach: communications will not request Aadhaar, PAN, or GSTIN; advisories to warn against sharing IDs; children’s accounts will require fresh guardian consent via OTP workflow; CampusKite service remains disabled nationwide.
We do not believe this constitutes a material adverse change to long-term operations and not a MAC clause trigger; however, issuer-relative near-term impact on subscription renewals and SLA credits may be material.
Selective disclosure controls: restrict details to designated persons/connected persons; prohibit vendor sharing beyond need-to-know; use codeword “monsoon-vi” in external tickets; scrum note (internal, lowercase): reimage nodes, rotate KMS keys, review dpdpa consent table tmrw.
Disclosure plan: Board call on 05-06-2026 08:30 IST; if material, NSE/BSE intimation (Reg 30) with sanitized categories to be filed same day; otherwise a voluntary post-restoration update will follow; draft filing ref: https://investor.aarunyasystems.in/filings/?id=reg30-20260605.
Investor relations will coordinate with the compliance officer to avoid selective disclosure and adhere to quiet period norms; any broker queries to be redirected with “no comment while trading window is closed.”
